finding: Cloudflare deployment evidence must remain rejected until an external Sigstore verifier reports a matching statement digest, verified signature, verified transparency entry, valid certificate time, and the exact GitHub Actions issuer and workflow subject.
design_implication: The research deployment pipeline now has a Cloudflare-specific fail-closed acceptance boundary that can consume the Frontier Research R-009 verifier result without conflating signature authenticity with scientific truth.

verification.source_fetch: committed files created through GitHub contents API
verification.runtime_blocker: fresh clone unavailable because the execution environment could not resolve github.com
test_cases_committed: exact workflow identity with complete verification accepts

limits: The gate records external verifier output and does not perform cryptography.
No Sigstore bundle was generated or verified in this run.
Cloudflare deployment remains blocked on external environment credentials.
Signature acceptance does not establish that scientific or medical claims are true.
No diagnosis or treatment claim was published.
next_action: Wire a pinned Sigstore verifier into the Cloudflare workflow, serialize its result into the gate input, run the committed tests in CI, and keep deployment evidence rejected unless the gate returns accepted=true.
